name: Willow Springs Golf Course
about: Willow Springs Golf Course is an 18-hole, par-62 links-style course in West Friendship, MD, designed by architect Al Janis to offer a challenging yet visually appealing experience for all skill levels. The facility features a 4,500 square foot clubhouse built in 2016, styled as a modern barn, which serves as a central hub for guests and can be reconfigured for various events. On-site amenities include a pro shop, snack bar, indoor golf simulator, and seasonal indoor hitting bays, complemented by a driving range and multiple league programs. The venue offers a comprehensive golf experience combining traditional play with modern indoor technology and a range of instructional programs.
why_golfers_like_it: Comprehensive indoor simulator facility with league opportunities
Scottish-links style course offering a unique and challenging layout
Full range of amenities including pro shop, snack bar, and event space
Flexible clubhouse capable of accommodating outings, events, and weddings
Multiple instructional programs and private lessons available

food_and_drink.notes: No specific mention of alcohol service, BYOB not indicated

simulator_hardware.notes: Simulator details not specified on website

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't explicitly state whether clubs are provided, but it's standard practice to bring your own equipment for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has an on-site snack bar (Dexter's Caddyshack), but doesn't mention a policy on outside food or drinks.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: Standard simulator play typically takes 2-3 hours for 18 holes, but actual time may vary based on pace of play and number of players.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Willow Springs offers junior golf programs, TGA camps, and family leagues, making it suitable for younger golfers.
